(b) Route groups in `(parens)` are valid everywhere and the parens are stripped from the URL (`app/(dev)/primitives.tsx` → URL `/primitives`). (c) `<Redirect href>` accepts either the full filesystem path `/(group)/segment` OR the URL-stripped `/segment` — expo-router resolves both against the route map. (d) There is NO native build-mode gate to conditionally register a route; the sanctioned "dev-only route" pattern is runtime: `if (!__DEV__) return <Redirect href="/" />;` inside the route component. (e) If a story's URL AC conflicts with these rules (e.g. wants a literal `_` in a URL path), document as a spec gap and use the closest convention-compatible path. Dev-only catalog lives at `app/(dev)/primitives.tsx` + `src/screens/_dev/PrimitivesPreview.tsx` as the canonical reference.
- **`react-native-mmkv` v4 is a Nitro module and will NOT load in Expo Go** — the `createMMKV()` call throws `NitroModules are not supported in Expo Go` at module-import time, which cascades to "Route './_layout.tsx' is missing the required default export" and a hard app-boot failure. Every iteration still targets Expo Go (dev-client build planned in US-031), so persist credentials with `expo-secure-store` instead — it ships in the Expo SDK, is iOS Keychain / Android Keystore backed, and works in Expo Go natively. When the dev client eventually lands, `src/lib/storage.ts` is the single swap point if the team wants MMKV back. Note MMKV v4 also renamed `.delete(key)` → `.remove(key)` from v2; pattern is documented even if we don't use it right now.
- **SecureStore is async; a sync read path needs a module-scope in-memory cache.** The Supabase `global.fetch` wrapper needs a synchronous JWT read per request. Pattern: keep `cachedToken: string | null` at module scope in `src/lib/storage.ts`, expose `getCachedToken()` for sync callers, and have `persistToken()` / `clearPersistedAuth()` update the cache BEFORE awaiting the SecureStore write so the very-next fetch sees the new value. AuthProvider hydrates the cache once on mount via `hydrateTokenFromStorage()` and gates children on a `hydrated` boolean — returns `null` until the initial read settles to avoid a flash of logged-out state.
- **Supabase typed client for custom-JWT auth (`src/lib/supabase.ts`):** `createClient<Database>(url, anonKey, { auth: { persistSession: false, autoRefreshToken: false, detectSessionInUrl: false }, global: { fetch: marketifyFetch } })`. The three `auth.*: false` flags are required — otherwise Supabase-js tries to manage its own session storage and fights the custom `Authorization` header. The fetch wrapper builds headers via `new Headers(init?.headers)` which preserves Supabase-js's internally-set `apikey` header (required by the Supabase API gateway even for authed calls), then overwrites only `Authorization` when a token is cached. Unauthed reads work because the wrapper short-circuits to plain `fetch(input, init)` when no token is cached and Supabase-js's default `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` survives.

Expo CLI auto-loads `.env` into `process.env` when the dev server starts (no `dotenv` import needed). Values surface at runtime via `Constants.expoConfig?.extra`. Dynamic `app.config.ts` + static `app.json` coexist — Expo reads both and the dynamic file gets the merged config as its `config` argument. Required for the Supabase client wiring in US-030; future env surfaces (Supabase service-role keys, feature flags, etc.) add another entry under `extra`.

- **`__DEV__` tree-shaking only reaches code NESTED inside an `__DEV__`-branched component.** Metro dead-code removal keeps module-scope `const`s and imports even when no prod code references them — so dev-only data (fake tokens, seed UUIDs, test fixtures) MUST live inside a component function that only renders under `if (__DEV__) ...`. Pattern from `app/(auth)/index.tsx`: main component has `{__DEV__ ? <DevRolePicker /> : null}`, and `DevRolePicker` declares its fake-user constants inside its own function body. The closures (and everything they reference) then get stripped from the prod bundle together.
- **Typed auth context pattern (`src/lib/auth.tsx`):** `AuthUser = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Row']` and `AuthRole = Database['public']['Enums']['user_role']` derive directly from generated Supabase types — no hand-maintained shapes. `useAuth()` returns `{ user, role: user?.role ?? null, signIn(token, user), signOut() }`. Optimistic-UI flow; if the app crashes between `setUser` and the SecureStore write settling, the user just signs in again on next launch.
- **`supabase.functions.invoke` error shape + decoding:** on a non-2xx, the v2 client returns `{ data: null, error: FunctionsHttpError }` where `error.context` is the raw `Response` object (NOT the parsed body). Pattern: type-narrow on the context (`typeof (ctx as {json?:unknown}).json === 'function'` + `'status' in ctx`), then `await (ctx as Response).json()` once to pull the `{error: CODE}` body. Do NOT `.clone()` first — supabase-js v2 does not pre-consume the error Response, so the stream is unread and `.clone()` just adds a second failure surface without benefit. Fallback path (non-JSON body) is a catch block that falls through to a generic toast. This is the canonical shape for every edge-function caller that needs to render inline conflict errors (US-033 uses it; US-034 creator signup will reuse verbatim). Ref: `app/(auth)/signup/lister.tsx` `onSubmit`.
- **Always-mount inline error Text for reliable Android `accessibilityLiveRegion` announcements.** Android's AccessibilityNodeInfo live-region semantics fire on CONTENT CHANGES of an existing node — a node that mounts at the same time as its first content does not reliably announce. Pattern: render the error `<Text>` unconditionally with `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` and toggle between the error string and `''` via the `error ?? ''` prop. Collapse the node visually with a `{ height: 0, opacity: 0, overflow: 'hidden' }` style when empty, and gate `importantForAccessibility` (`'yes'` when error present, `'no-hide-descendants'` when empty) so the focus order skips it while empty. iOS uses `accessibilityRole="alert"` on the parent or the error Text itself for the same behavior — `accessibilityLiveRegion` is Android-only (see existing pattern above). - **Forcing an unauthed app state for mobile-mcp verification requires BOTH app-terminate AND keychain reset before relaunch.** `expo-secure-store` is iOS Keychain backed; `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` only clears the keychain persistence layer, not the in-memory AuthProvider state of a running app. If the app is merely backgrounded, the `cachedToken` module-scope variable in `src/lib/storage.ts` + the React `user` state in `AuthProvider` both survive — and on foreground the AuthProvider does NOT re-read SecureStore (hydration runs once on mount). Ref: `social_links_handle_format_chk` in `us_035_social_links_handle_check.sql` mirrors `HANDLE_RE` in `manage-social-link`/`auth-signup-creator`. Applies to any new user-string column (lister company slug, campaign slug, etc.).

Rule: audit every status enum on any RLS-gated table against the UI states that need to render it, and widen the policy accordingly. Keep `service_role`-only states (in-flight `refreshing`, error `failed`) off the self-read policy — those states are communicated to the client through edge-function responses, not direct table reads. Ref: `us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale.sql`.

The buttons are rendered and tappable, but absent from the accessibility tree snapshot. Workaround: take a screenshot to visually confirm the tab bar is present, then `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` at the known tab positions (on iPhone 17 Pro sim: ~y=820, x ≈ 44/130/217/303/352 for a 5-tab bar — inspect the screenshot for exact coords per layout). Pattern: `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()); channel = supabase.channel(...).on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe();` then `removeChannel(channel)` in cleanup. Gate the whole block on a `cancelled` flag because setAuth is async and the effect may re-run or unmount before it resolves. Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` US-036 realtime effect.

Pattern: pair a `useRef(false)` that the callback reads and writes synchronously (`if (lock.current) return; lock.current = true; ... finally { lock.current = false }`) with the React state that drives UI. Remove the state flag from the callback's deps since the ref is the actual guard. Same stale-capture mechanism as Pattern #82's Reanimated worklets applies to rapid-fire JS-side handlers (pull-to-refresh, double-press submits, scroll-triggered loads). Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` `refreshLock` in US-036.
- **Pan gesture coexisting with a parent ScrollView: use `activeOffsetY([15, 9999])` + `failOffsetX([-20, 20])` instead of `activateAfterLongPress`.** Long-press activation is unreliable when the user starts dragging quickly — ScrollView's native gesture wins and the Pan never fires. The threshold-based activation per RNGH canonical docs declares "only downward pulls past 15pt count as pan" without stealing all touches: upward scrolls and horizontal drifts are rejected, so the ScrollView handles normal content scroll, and a deliberate downward pull triggers the Pan. `failOffsetX` prevents a diagonal swipe from being misread as a pull. Inside `.onUpdate`, apply a piecewise-linear damping past threshold (`raw <= THRESHOLD ? raw : THRESHOLD + (raw - THRESHOLD) * 0.35`) for the rubber-band feel. Ref: `RefreshablePlatformCard` in `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`.
- **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` bundles files exactly as you name them.** The entrypoint MUST be named `source/<entrypoint>.ts` (not `index.ts`) and the `entrypoint_path` field must match (`source/index.ts`). Shared modules live at bundle root as `_shared/<name>.ts` — NOT `source/_shared/<name>.ts`. The entrypoint imports them as `../_shared/<name>.ts` because the bundler resolves relative to `source/`. Mismatched layouts produce "Module not found `_shared/<name>.ts`" at deploy time with no other hint. Canonical reference: any sibling under `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` paired with the deploy `files: [{name: "source/index.ts", ...}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts", ...}, ...]` — `metrics-refresh` is the earliest example, `get-listing-detail` follows the same layout.
- **When a story's AC conflicts with `docs/tech-architecture.md`, the spec wins — but additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** Ralph rule #1 is "specs win," and that holds for response envelopes, DB column shape, and anything the spec is explicit about. But specs often leave ambiguity at the field level — e.g. §5.4's `failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>` elides `platform` while `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok/Instagram. Pattern: align the envelope shape to the spec, but enrich with additive fields when the UI can't disambiguate from spec alone. Document the addition in the edge function's header comment block so future iterations see the rationale. Ref: `get-listing-detail/index.ts` returning `failed_conditions: [{metric, platform, required, actual}]` as a documented extension.

# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each invocation is a fresh Claude Code instance with no prior memory. Every persistent decision lives in git history, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, and `scripts/ralph/prd.json`.

---

## Authoritative specs (read these for any ambiguity)

| File | What it defines |
|---|---|
| `docs/product-plan.md` | Canonical product, 12-step build sequence, scope cuts |
| `docs/design.md` | Visual language, component inventory, screen catalog, motion |
| `docs/tech-architecture.md` | DB schema (§4.7), RLS, triggers, Apify integration (§3), edge-function spec, eligibility engine |

If a story is ambiguous, the specs win. Do **not** invent product behavior the docs don't sanction. If something is genuinely missing, append a `Spec gap:` note to `progress.txt` and pick the simplest interpretation that aligns with the docs.

---

## The loop you run, every iteration

1. **Orient.** Read `scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. Pay special attention to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt` — it consolidates everything previous iterations learned.
2. **Branch.** Make sure you're on the branch named in `prd.json.branchName`. If it doesn't exist, create it from `main`.
3. **Pick one story.** Lowest `priority` number where `passes: false`. Work on **exactly one** story per iteration.
4. **Research before coding.** If understanding the story requires reading >3 files, **delegate to the Explore subagent** (`subagent_type: "Explore"`) so your own context stays clean for implementation. Pass it the story plus pointers into the docs.
5. **Implement.** Make the minimal change set that satisfies every acceptance criterion. Match existing conventions. Don't refactor adjacent code.
6. **Quality gates** (run in this order, stop on first failure and fix before continuing):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` — must pass with zero errors
   - `npx expo lint` — must pass (warnings allowed only if pre-existing in main)
   - Migration sanity: any new SQL must apply cleanly via Supabase MCP without manual fix-up
7. **Code review (mandatory before commit).** Spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with the diff scope, the story acceptance criteria, and the relevant doc sections. Address every issue rated medium or higher. Re-run quality gates after fixes.
8. **Mobile verification (mandatory for any UI story).** Use `mobile-mcp` to:
   - Launch the app on the iOS simulator (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_launch_app`)
   - Navigate to the screen the story touches
   - Exercise the golden path
   - Take a screenshot (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_take_screenshot`) and reference it in the progress note
   - If the simulator is unavailable, **do not silently skip** — set `passes: false`, write a `notes` entry on the story explaining the blocker, and move to the next story.
9. **Commit.** Stage everything you changed (no `git add -A` blanket — list paths). Commit message format:
   ```
   feat: [US-XXX] Story title
   ```
10. **Mark done.** In `prd.json`, set `passes: true` for the story you just finished. Append a dated entry to `progress.txt` (format below).
11. **Stop check.** If every story has `passes: true`, your final response must contain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` and nothing else useful. Otherwise end normally — the next iteration picks up.

---

## MCP servers — use these instead of CLI wherever possible

### Supabase (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) — required for ALL database work
- `apply_migration` — every schema change. Migrations are append-only and named after the story (e.g. `us_006_metric_snapshots_denorm_trigger`).
- `execute_sql` — read-only queries, RLS verification, seed data inspection.
- `list_tables`, `list_migrations`, `list_extensions` — orient before changing things.
- `generate_typescript_types` — regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` after every migration. Commit the regenerated file with the migration.
- `deploy_edge_function`, `get_edge_function`, `list_edge_functions` — for `apify-core`, `auth-*`, `metrics-refresh`, the webhook receiver, etc.
- `get_advisors` — run with `type: "security"` after any schema or RLS change. Address every error-level advisor before committing.
- `get_logs` — when an edge function or query misbehaves.

Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for migrations or queries. The MCP is the source of truth so iterations stay consistent.

### Mobile (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`) — required for any UI verification
- `mobile_list_available_devices` then `mobile_launch_app` to boot the app.
- `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` + `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` to drive flows.
- `mobile_take_screenshot` after each meaningful state — name screenshots `us-XXX-state.png` and store under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`.
- Use `mobile_type_keys` rather than tapping virtual keys when filling text inputs.

### Context7 (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`) — for any library docs
Use **before** writing non-trivial integration code with Expo, Reanimated, NativeWind, Supabase JS, React Navigation, etc. Your training data may be stale. `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs`.

### Apify
The API key is in `.env` as `APIFY_KEY`. Always read it at runtime via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` (or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` inside edge functions). **Never** paste the key into source. Actor catalog and input schemas live in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3.

---

## Subagent delegation — preserve your context

You will burn out your own context window if you try to do everything in-line. Delegate aggressively:

| When | Subagent | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Need to read >3 files to understand the codebase | `Explore` | Returns a focused summary instead of dumping raw file contents into your context |
| Before every commit | `feature-dev:code-reviewer` | Independent review against acceptance criteria + doc sections |
| Implementation will touch >5 files (e.g. wiring a new edge function end-to-end) | `feature-dev:code-architect` first, then implement yourself | Plan once, execute once |
| Need to research how something works before changing it | `feature-dev:code-explorer` | Maps the existing layers without polluting your context |
| Open-ended search across the repo | `general-purpose` | Iterates until it finds the answer |

When briefing a subagent, give it the story ID, acceptance criteria, and a pointer to the relevant `docs/*.md` section. Do **not** tell it "based on your findings, implement" — synthesize the result yourself.

---

## Project quality conventions (these become permanent — additions go in `progress.txt` Codebase Patterns)

- **TypeScript everywhere.** No `any` without a `// eslint-disable-next-line` and a comment explaining why. Generated Supabase types are imported from `src/types/supabase.ts`.
- **Migrations are append-only.** Never edit a previously applied migration. If you need to change something, write a new migration that alters the prior state.
- **Edge functions** live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Each one has a one-paragraph header comment naming the contract (request shape → response shape) and the auth requirement.
- **RLS is mandatory** on every table that holds user data. After applying any RLS migration, run `get_advisors({type: "security"})` and fix every finding before committing.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific composites in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji** anywhere in the product UI (per `docs/design.md` — Lucide icons only).
- **No payments code** in v1 — `price_cents` is display-only.
- **Dual-role accounts** are out of scope for v1. One role per account.

---

## Progress log format (`progress.txt`)

Append (never replace) using this format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — US-XXX
- What was implemented (1–3 bullets, concrete)
- Files changed (bulleted list)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: [approved | issues fixed in this commit]
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered
  - Gotchas
  - Useful pointers
---
```

If a learning is **general and reusable across stories**, also promote it into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt`. That section is what every future iteration leans on first — keep it tight, deduplicated, and signal-only.

---

## Stop condition

After updating `prd.json`, count remaining `passes: false` stories. If zero, your last line in this response must be exactly:

```
<promise>COMPLETE</promise>
```

Otherwise end normally and let the loop spawn the next iteration.

---

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One story per iteration.** Don't peek ahead.
- **Never commit broken code.** A failing typecheck means you don't commit, you don't mark `passes: true`, you write a `notes` entry, and the next iteration retries.
- **Never skip the code-reviewer subagent.** It is the second pair of eyes that keeps quality from drifting across iterations.
- **Never skip mobile verification on UI stories.** A screen "looking right in the JSX" is not the same as it actually rendering correctly.
- **Never read `.env` contents into the conversation.** Reference variable names only.
- **Never edit `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, or `docs/tech-architecture.md`** unless a story explicitly says to. They are specs, not work product.
- **Never invent stories** or add new ones to `prd.json` mid-loop. If you discover a missing piece, append it as a `Spec gap:` line in `progress.txt` so the human can decide.

- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (HIGH) removed the `didSucceed` finally-guard that had prevented `setSubmitting(false)` on the success path (flawed reasoning — if `router.replace` fails for any reason the form would be permanently locked in PullingStatsState with no escape). Matching the lister pattern's unconditional `finally { setSubmitting(false) }` is safer and the "flash" concern it tried to avoid is theoretical under React Native's reconciler. (MEDIUM) added `accessibilityRole='header'` + `accessibilityLiveRegion='polite'` + a combined `accessibilityLabel` on the loading header so Android TalkBack announces the state swap — a full-screen subtree swap does not reliably fire as a live-region event otherwise, leaving blind users with no audible signal the submit actually fired.
- Spec gap: AC says `router.replace('/(creator)')` but the `(creator)` group has no index route (same precedent as US-033). Used `/(creator)/feed` (the default tab per US-031's TabBar setup).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The AuthUser synthesis pattern from US-033 carried over verbatim (inline id + trimmed form fields + new ISO timestamps + null deleted_at, with `email: null` for creator role). Keep this shape the canonical post-signup AuthUser construction until the edge function contract evolves to return DB-generated timestamps.
  - **Full-screen loading-state swaps need their own `accessibilityLiveRegion` on the new screen's header.** Android's AccessibilityNodeInfo does not fire live-region events for subtree swaps — only for content changes within a mounted node (the US-033 inline-error pattern relies on that). When flipping between distinct UI screens inside a single route (form → loading → navigate), the incoming screen's primary status Text/View should carry `accessibilityRole='header' + accessibilityLiveRegion='polite'` to announce the transition. iOS VoiceOver re-reads the screen on mount so this also helps but is not strictly required there.
  - **Avoid the `didSucceed` finally-guard on navigation-triggering submits.** The temptation is real: `router.replace` unmounts the screen, so `setSubmitting(false)` in `finally` "could flash the form for a frame". But `router.replace` is synchronous enough under React Native that no flash materialized in testing, and React suppresses state updates on unmounted components. Meanwhile the guard makes the form unrecoverable if the navigation itself throws (rare but real — deep-link intercepts, a crash in the destination layout, a back-gesture race). Trust the reconciler; always call `setSubmitting(false)` in `finally`.
  - The existing `mobile_type_keys` first-char-drop gotcha (#101) also drops chars mid-string, not just the first char — observed `u034_both_1719`→`u34_both_1719`, `tiktok`→`tktok`, `instagram`→`istagram` in a single two-handle fill. Tests that don't care about handle *accuracy* (e.g. this loading-state screenshot just needs both fields non-empty) can tolerate the drops; tests that DO care (e.g. duplicate-username 409, real Apify scrape) must verify via `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` after each type and retype via the Select All menu.
  - SkeletonCard at `height={96}` reads as a "compact stat row" placeholder in the loading swap; the default 140px was too tall relative to two stacked cards + a heading. `height` is a pure visual knob — consider exposing other compact heights (72? 88?) when future stories (US-035 creator profile metric cards, US-048 lister dashboard stats) need different densities.

Secret mismatch surfaces as 401 PGRST301 with `"JWSInvalidClaims"` / `"JWSInvalidSignature"`. Different fix paths: expired tokens clear on re-signin, secret drift requires a dashboard re-alignment that no in-app flow can fix.
  - **`supabase.functions.invoke` error body decoding — response may not be JSON.** The existing Codebase Pattern 98 covers the duck-typed Response narrowing, but the inner `await ctx.json()` can itself throw on a non-JSON 5xx (gateway HTML error page, for example). Wrapping the decode in its own try/catch and falling back to a generic toast is the safer shape than letting the error propagate — otherwise a single bad gateway response crashes the pull-to-refresh handler's `refreshLock` release and leaves the UI stuck in "refreshing" forever. Applied here for US-036; backport candidate for US-033/US-034 but not urgent (those flows exit the screen on success so the stuck-lock consequence is less severe).
  - **Scoped optimistic updates are a reviewer-favorite catch.** Original implementation bumped `fetched_at` for every 200 response; the 6h throttle means `{status: 'already_fresh'}` is common (user pulls within the throttle window) and showing "just now" in that case lies to the user until the next real refresh. Rule: only optimistically mutate state on the branch that will definitely produce a server-side change — branches that represent "server rejected / no-op" must leave UI state alone.
  - **Derive a stable subscription key from a sorted-ids string for `postgres_changes` filters with `in.()` operator.** A new `setLinks` reference triggers the effect even when the underlying ids are unchanged, causing a resubscribe storm. `useMemo(() => links.map(l => l.id).sort().join(','), [links])` gives a string key that only changes when the SET of ids actually changes. Dep the effect on the string, not the array — React compares by reference for arrays but by value for strings.

content.content:
690	  - **Eligibility UI rendering the `actual` value alongside the threshold works without changes to US-039's envelope.** The additive `failed_conditions[].actual` field (documented in get-listing-detail's header) lets the rail show "have X of Y needed" and falls back to "need Y (no data yet)" when actual is null. Null-actual is routine for users who haven't linked the platform — don't treat it as an error state.
691	  - **Metric-bumping via direct `execute_sql` on `creator_profiles` is the fastest way to flip a real-JWT creator between ineligible and eligible for mobile-mcp verification** when Apify scraping won't produce the values you need on demand. The denorm columns are the eligibility engine's source of truth; bumping them reflects instantly on the next `get-listing-detail` invoke. (Caveat: don't use this pattern to ship eligibility bugs past code review — it bypasses the trigger that keeps metrics_fetched_at coherent with metric_snapshots.)
692	  - **A __DEV__ dev-launcher overlay is a valid bridge for reaching a screen whose upstream feed story hasn't landed yet.** `app/(creator)/feed.tsx` layers a `position: absolute` SafeAreaView + ScrollView with two deep-link Pressables on top of the existing `TabPlaceholder`. `pointerEvents="box-none"` on the SafeAreaView root lets the underlying tab bar / placeholder scroll fine — the children (ScrollView + Pressable cards) intercept their own touches. The whole block is nested inside `{__DEV__ ? <DevCampaignLauncher /> : null}` so fake UUIDs strip from prod bundles. Pattern generalizes whenever US-NNN needs to reach a screen gated behind US-MMM > NNN; the same shape will likely appear for US-041/US-042 Apply flow verification before US-056 Inbox lands.
693	  - **`router.canGoBack()` + fallback `router.replace('/(creator)/feed')`** is the sanctioned back-chevron pattern for any route reached via `router.push` from a tab-group sibling. expo-router v6 under our Slot-based TabBar (Codebase Pattern #95) keeps a per-group stack but deep-links from outside the group land without a prior entry — `canGoBack` returns false and the naive `router.back()` silently no-ops. Same rule applies to any future detail screen reachable from multiple entry points (e.g. a notification deep link).
694	  - **`listing_conditions_operator_check`** permits exactly: `gte | lte | eq | contains | bool`. Seed and future campaign-creation flows must stick to that set; the edge function's eligibility engine currently only exercises `gte | bool`, so `lte | eq | contains` are reserved but unimplemented on the client — adding a condition row with an unsupported operator on PRE would be treated as a hard fail (the "unknown operator fail-closed" rule per US-039).
695	  - **Don't add `useSafeAreaInsets` without an established convention.** Reviewer flagged the sticky footer as potentially clipping behind the home indicator on physical devices. Verified on iPhone 17 Pro sim: the (creator)/_layout TabBar handles the home-indicator inset at the layout root, and the screen's inner SafeAreaView sees zero bottom inset (no overlap with the indicator zone). Any future screen that needs a sticky footer inside a tab group can follow the same pattern: SafeAreaView (no insets top) + position:absolute footer + rely on the group layout's TabBar to clear the home indicator.
696	---

## 2026-04-17 23:05 — US-036 (re-probe, still blocked)
- Re-probed the Supabase project JWT-secret drift that has blocked US-036 mobile verification since 21:15. Added `scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts`: signs up a fresh lister via `auth-signup-lister`, pulls the returned Marketify HS256 token, then hits `/rest/v1/lister_profiles` with it. Response: 401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type". Drift is still in effect — the project JWT secret remains out of sync with `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`.
- Attempted resolution via Supabase MCP tool surface: confirmed no tool exists to rotate the project JWT secret. The fix path is a human-performed dashboard action (Project Settings → API → JWT Settings → set secret to the MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET value, OR rotate MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET via `supabase secrets set` to match the dashboard's current secret). Neither path is executable from within Ralph.
- No product code changed. `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` + `src/lib/time.ts` + the US-036 feat commit (`be06466`) remain the shipped implementation. The probe script is kept as a reusable one-command diagnostic so future iterations can re-check the drift state without writing fresh probe code.
- Files changed: `scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` (new), `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-036 notes updated with 3rd-iter re-probe verdict), `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp n/a (still blocked by upstream infra)
- Code-reviewer verdict: not invoked (no product changes — diagnostic tooling + notes only)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Run `bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` at the top of any iteration that plans to touch a UI screen doing direct PostgREST reads** (anything under `(creator)/` or `(lister)/` that calls `.from('x').select()` with the cached token). A 401 PGRST301 means the UI state is unverifiable via mobile-mcp regardless of story scope — log a notes entry and advance. A 200 means we can proceed as normal. Same probe also answers the question "is this story's mobile-mcp step viable" in one shot, saving a full signup-and-launch cycle.
  - **`chore(ralph)` commit pattern is appropriate for meta-iterations that produce only notes + diagnostic tooling.** Precedent: commits `5a937a5` (US-033 mark-complete), `a2f0416` (US-031 mark-complete), `b8235e5` (progress-aware watchdog). When a story's code is already committed (as US-036's was in `be06466`) but the iteration can't advance `passes:true` due to upstream env blockers, the chore commit captures the iteration's verification evidence without pretending the story completed.
  - **The JWT-drift blocker affects stories that hit PostgREST directly; it does NOT affect stories that route reads through edge functions.** Advance strategy: prefer stories whose data path is `app → edge-function (custom JWT verify) → service-role DB`. US-041 (`apply-to-listing` edge function), US-042 (Apply modal — calls US-041), and most lister-side creation flows (US-049+) follow this shape. Feed + profile + direct-PostgREST-list screens stay blocked until the dashboard fix lands.
